title: (Artifact) Thoth 2.0: Advancing an RSL Tool for Enhanced Snowballing Support
author: dos Santos Comis, Diego
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Thoth 2.0 is an open-source tool designed to assist researchers in conducting Systematic Literature Reviews (SLRs), with a focus on automating the Snowballing technique. The tool supports both backward and forward citation analysis using CrossRef API, offers collaborative project management, quality assessment, and structured data extraction, all within an intuitive web-based interface. 
  Repository:    GitHub (development version):  https://github.com/Thoth2023/thoth-remake     
  Public Instance:     https://thoth-slr.com  
  Emblems Claimed:    - Available: The artifact is public, versioned, and has a valid open-source LICENSE.   - Functional: The artifact includes installation and execution instructions via Docker. The application can be tested through a public instance or installed locally as described in the README. 
  Required Skills:    - Basic understanding of Docker and command-line tools   - Familiarity with Laravel/PHP (for advanced customization) 
  Required Resources:    - Docker Desktop (Windows/Linux/macOS)   - Minimum 4GB RAM, 1GB free disk space   - Internet access for external APIs (CrossRef, Algolia) 
  Instructions to Run:    Follow the README steps at https://github.com/Thoth2023/thoth-remake.   Alternatively, access the tool via the live instance: https://thoth-slr.com 
  Associated Paper:    https://jems3.sbc.org.br/submissions/11616

Thoth 2.0 is an open-source tool designed to assist researchers in conducting Systematic Literature Reviews (SLRs), with a focus on automating the Snowballing technique. The tool supports both backward and forward citation analysis using CrossRef API, offers collaborative project management, quality assessment, and structured data extraction, all within an intuitive web-based interface.

The application can be tested through a public instance or installed locally as described in the README. 
  Required Skills:    - Basic understanding of Docker and command-line tools   - Familiarity with Laravel/PHP (for advanced customization) 
  Required Resources:    - Docker Desktop (Windows/Linux/macOS)   - Minimum 4GB RAM, 1GB free disk space   - Internet access for external APIs (CrossRef, Algolia)
